title: Study of the (alpha,2alpha) reaction at 700 MeV

x4freetext: Study of the (alpha,2alpha) reaction at 700 MeV

x4freetext: primary particle telescopes and two secondary
telescopes for beam monitoring. The two particle
telescopes of the primary detection system were
oriented at 35 degrees and 52.5 degrees. A particle
traversing the higher energy telescope (35 degrees)
was defined by coincident signals in the plastic four
scintillators. A bending magnet 45 cm wide and 91 cm
long was located between two sets of wire spark
chambers with magnetostrictive readouts. A spark
chamber set consisted of three wire planes for
horizontal coordinates, and two wire planes for
vertical coordinates. A particle traversing the lower
energy telescope (52.5 degrees) was defined by
coincident signals in plastic scintillators rs A1, A2
and A3, where A3 was the first detector in the range
telescope. One set of wire spark chambers was located
between A1 and A2, defining the scattering angle of
the particle. The range telescope included six 0.08
